pcr: EPD Hub Core PCR v1.2 (2025-03-24); cPCR EN 16757
notes: Cover title reads '40 MPa High Performance Concrete Melbourne'; the Product specification section (p4) gives the base product name without the location suffix — product_name follows p4, with 'Melbourne' captured separately as location.production.

Scope text on p3 prints 'Cradle-to-gate (A1-3)' (non-standard abbreviation) vs 'A1-A3' used throughout the rest of the document (environmental data summary p4, impact tables p13-18); normalized to 'A1-A3' in the scope field here.
A4-A5 (transport/installation) and B1-B7 (use stage) are all ND per the system boundary table (p5) and the 'Modules not declared' text (p11) — narrower scope than EPDs that declare A4-A5.

EPD verifier named is Vera Durão, acting for EPD Hub Limited (p3); the Internal/External verification checkboxes on p3 render without a visible check mark in extracted text, so 'external' is inferred from the named third-party verifier (same verifier as EPD Hub example HUB-5943).

location.sites (8 sites) sourced from the site-grouping paragraph on p6, which also confirms the state as Victoria; place of production ('Melbourne') and country context appear separately on p3's general information section.

Product is a single declared result averaged by volume across 8 grouped Melbourne-metro sites (Deer Park, Tullamarine, Somerton, Sunbury, Wollert, Melton, West Melbourne, West Melbourne (wet)); reported GWP variation across sites for A1-A3 is -0.4% to +1.3% (p6).

EN 15804+A1/CML table (p17) gives Global Warming Pot. A1-A3 = 297, effectively identical to the +A2 figure — captured as gwp_a1a3_alt to power the 'not comparable' methodology warning. A separate GWP-GHG table (p18) also = 297 but per schema is never used for gwp_a1a3_alt.
